tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: I can finish this assignment in minutes with no other deadlines looming, so I'll just push through the last 10% now.
The deadline was 10 minutes away, but I finished the assignment early and submitted it on time. That close call makes me feel a bit relieved but also anxious about my next task.

desires: Finish the assignment and hit submit before the clock ticks to 13:30 so I don't have to deal with the anxiety of a missed deadline or looking incompetent.
Get this over with immediately so I can stop feeling that tight knot in my stomach about the 'close call' from yesterday.
Avoid any friction with the professor; just give them exactly what they asked for and disappear back into my work without needing to explain myself.

goals.description: Submit the Graded assignment immediately to lock in the win and stop the anxiety loop; I can't risk a last-second glitch or awkward follow-up with Prof. Assignment.
goals.rationale: My 'achievement' axis screams for this closure right now. The 'avoidant' trait makes me terrified of the friction that comes from explaining delays or dealing with potential negative feedback, so finishing this cleanly is my only option. My 'impulsive' nature means I won't wait for perfect conditions; I'll just hit submit to get the knot out of my stomach.

agent.last_reasoning: The submission already hit the LMS; reworking now wastes energy. My impulsive urge to 'fix' it conflicts with the achieved goal. I'll wait out the 10-minute window to avoid friction, then message cohort_3 about the win instead of stressing.
